Alright, we're going to the next tonality here. We have what's called your presupposing tone. And this one is one of my favorite ones of all because it's so subtle, it gets so powerful and people don't really even know what's going on. And by the way, almost I don't even realize what I'm doing. I use it so much that my own family, like my kids work here, they laugh when they hear me using it because they just like, wow, you know that you just did that without even thinking about what do you mean? You just use your presupposing tone. Let me tell you what it is. You get to a certain point in the sales encounter where you've made an airtight logical case, you've filled in the blanks, you've done your aloof series, it's really a scream from the hilltop airtight logical case. You've also moved them emotionally about the same items, right? You're going to use your words to create that logical case, your tonality and future pacing to move someone emotionally, right? But there's a certain point towards the end of the sale where we've sought to really actively engage in heavy future pacing and using a process called looping, which you're going to learn later on in these modules here. But what we do, what I do really well here is I apply a tonality for my looping and future pacing called a presupposing tone. What I do is it's almost like I say, it's so obvious, it's like beyond obvious. You call it almost a tonality of implied obviousness or presupposing. It's almost like they're called assuming the sale, this old sales phrase, right? But it's at a much higher level. Individed obvious, presupposing, meaning, Jim, you'll make money with it. Let's say I'm trying to talk about an investment, I'll say, you'll make money with it. It's beyond obvious, and it's beyond obvious. Now, Jim, you'll make money with it, but more importantly, what I could do if you're beyond this one transaction, there's almost like it's already a foregone conclusion. Obviously, you'll make money with it here. That's not even the question anymore. We've moved past that, Jim. But more importantly, and then I started explaining all the other things I can do for someone over the long term, and it's almost like, check, we both agree. Yes, it's an awesome product. Obviously, you love the product, Jim. Obviously, it's awesome. It's this way of talking, and you'll see when we really get to the scripting session here where you're just going to say, wow, this really makes sense, because you're essentially moving past the issue of whether or not something is awesome, whether or not it makes sense. Yes, it's beyond. We're past that. I know you love the product. More importantly, though, what I could do if you're over the long term, the way of this, that, customer service, other opportunities. So you move past something, and you check, yep, it's beyond obvious. Oh, please. We already, Jim, of course you'll make money here. But more importantly, that's the best way to explain this tonality. It kind of sounded a bit weird, but that's how I explain it. It's beyond that. Of course you'll make money here, Jim, but more importantly, and by almost pushing off, we already agree with that, Jim. Check. Now I move forward. Now let's talk about the bigger and better stuff. Our long-term relationship to the future, what I could do if you're over the long term by being in your life. Powerful, powerful tonality. All right? And now that takes us to the 10th tonality here, which is another one I truly favor. It's also a very important one here. Seriously. Another one is the eye care. I feel your pain. Or I used to call it the Bill Clinton tonality, because Bill Clinton was truly the master of this tonality. Bill, you know, remember Bill, right? Some of you might be too young, but when Bill was our president, boy, I got to tell you, you know, Bill Clinton would like, you know, he lied to us. He had sex in the Oval Office. He did all sorts of stuff, but we just like, thank you, Mr. President, because he had this way when Bill Clinton would look you in the eye or you'd shake his hand. You'd say, so tell me, how, how are you? You're like, oh my God, he feels my pain. He understands me. He gets me. It's a certain tonality. I care. I feel your pain. This is a crucial tonality when you are gathering intelligence and you say this when you know, so, you know, John, what's really keeping you up there? What's your biggest pain point here? Because you say, well, what's your biggest fear? And they'll say, screw you. You know, if you say that, they hate you. They'll say, Jim, what's your deepest worry right now? I care. I mean, it's not just, I really want to know. It's beyond that one. It's like, I care. I feel your pain. And as someone's responding, like, uh-huh, mm-hmm, I totally, uh-hmm. And this is that Bill Clinton was truly the master of this tonality. Okay? And it's called the I care. I feel your pain. And when you do that correctly, it allows you to dig into someone's pain points to find out exactly what really is kind of keeping them up in that, which sits the base of their skull and weighs them down like an anchor to do that and cover that without breaking rapport. You get it? Because if you say it the wrong way, they're gonna say, wow, screw you. What's keeping you up? What's keeping you up? They reverse on you and it's a damaging thing to the possibility of closing the sale. So you're gonna really make sure that when you're digging into some of those personal pain-oriented questions, you want to use that I care. I feel your pain tonality. Very powerful stuff. Now what I want to do here is stop. Want you to take a quiz and go through the exercise. Remember, these exercises are really important with tonality. And then when we come back, I'm gonna be cutting through a very special lesson here where I'm gonna show you some tricks of how to muster the right tonality by linking it to a specific body language movement. And each one of those movements is specific to you. So you get to figure out what your movements are and I'll show you exactly how to link them to the right tonalities and you can be blown away. First take your quiz and I will see you shortly after that. Take care.
